faqs.question: Can you match my existing siding style and color for repairs?
faqs.answer: Yes. We stock a wide range of James Hardie, vinyl, and cedar profiles. For older homes, we take a sample and source the closest match. Color-matched paint is always an option for seamless blending.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have wood rot behind my siding?
faqs.answer: Signs include soft or spongy siding when pressed, dark gray or black staining, paint peeling repeatedly in the same spot, or visible shrinkage and delamination. We do a free probe test during any estimate.

faqs.question: Will you help with an insurance claim for storm damage?
faqs.answer: Yes. We document damage with photos, produce a detailed damage report, and coordinate directly with your adjuster. Most storm damage claims for siding are covered under standard homeowners policies.

faqs.question: How much does siding repair cost in Seattle?
faqs.answer: Small repairs (1–3 boards, caulk failure) run $300–$800. Moderate repairs with rot remediation run $1,000–$3,500. Extensive damage may make full replacement more cost-effective — we'll advise honestly.
